text: Hello everyone, this is Dr. Gursiv Sandalas and today I am going to talk about robotic piloplasty using stealth placement of ports in the inguinal region which we did very decently. I was assisted by Dr. Vipul and by Dr. Takrooni who is our fellow in minimal access surgery from Egypt. It was a two-year-old girl child who had a history of pain in abdomen and was incidentally detected with the right PUG obstruction. So this is the port placement. Like I said, the patient was in semi-lateral position. Camera was in the left suprapubic region, just left of the midline. One port was in the right inguinal crease. One camera port was in the umbilicus and the assistant port was in the epigastrium.

The laparoscopic view shows the kidney from below, with the lower pole closest to the camera. The kidney is slightly rotated posteriorly, and the pelvis is centrally positioned in the surgical field. The hepatic flexure is adhered to the lateral abdominal wall, requiring careful dissection. A vein is visible leading to the lower pole, limiting further mobilization of the pelvis to avoid vascular damage.

The ureter is easily identified in the surgical field, facilitating straightforward dissection. The surgeon begins the PUJ anastomosis using robotic instruments, which maneuver efficiently in the limited space. The robotic fulcrum at the instrument tip allows precise movements, contrasting with the amplified hand movements in laparoscopy due to the abdominal wall fulcrum.

The surgeon demonstrates the range of movement of robotic instruments, performing hand-like suturing on the posterior wall. A stent is being placed, followed by the closure of the anterior wall. The robotic approach provides a different perspective compared to conventional port placement.

because we had some issue with the valve leakage from one of the ports causing suboptimal pneumoperitoneum in the beginning of the surgery and that consumed some time but otherwise I think in normal circumstances without that leakage we would have had a similar operating time as compared to conventional port placements and a similar quality of surgery at the end of the day and I don't think I think it's a good idea to maybe at least for the robotic to start doing these with stealth port placement we may not be able to do these laparoscopic but then again laparoscopic instruments are much smaller that we use in a two-year-old child will usually use a three millimeter instruments which anyway will leave much smaller scars. So the orals were started after four hours, patient discharged within forty-eight hours and I think it does offer superior cosmesis without taking away anything from the surgical experience.
